items.claim: Rodriguez has good foot pressure and power.
items.quote: Rodriguez, good striker, good foot pressure, good power, and good combinations.
He's got pretty good top pressure once he gets on top.

items.claim: Franco's previous opponents were not legitimate fighters.
items.quote: He's got a bunch of fights against people that are not fighters. Literally not fighters.

items.claim: Franco is not a good striker.
items.quote: he's not a good striker, guys. He's not.
he does struggle to close distance, which might be tough considering he's fighting a guy that's a skyscraper for the division.

items.claim: Ramirez has good top control and is a grinder.
items.quote: He has good top control, and he's a bit of a grinder.

items.claim: Ramirez is calm in bad positions on the ground.
items.quote: Very calm in bad positions. If you get him in a bad position, he doesn't freak out.
their durability doesn't seem to be there anymore because there was a time when Chase Hooper could just take an absolute beating and then pull out a sub. That's not the case anymore.

items.claim: Chase Hooper is a superior grappler to Ramirez.
items.quote: Ramirez is good on the ground but Chase is really really good on the ground.

items.claim: Melissanidis struggles when forced to fight off the back foot.
items.quote: if you really just press her and make her have to fight off the back foot, she does kind of lose some of that that that cleanness of the boxing.

items.claim: Melissanidis is vulnerable to being hit when pressured.
items.quote: she can be hit a lot when she is getting pressed.
